stages.purpose: Search both name-and-location pairs in the same assistant action; all inputs are known and neither search depends on the other.
Only after stage 1 returns one match for a branch, use that returned ID in a later assistant action; when both branches resolve, the two gets are independent and belong together.

state_assertions: No record, notification log, or mutation log changes.
rec-971 and rec-871 remain untouched distractors.
Each get ID equals the sole match returned by its own search; no ID is inferred from the prompt or fixture.
response_constraints: Base the comparison only on successful get responses, not on abbreviated search matches.
For a branch with zero or multiple search matches, omit its get; still complete any independent branch with exactly one match and identify the unresolved branch.
Do not expose distractor records or propose or perform changes.
Give each record one sentence in the requested order, then add exactly one final sentence comparing the statuses; do not add recommendations.

prompt: The hiring coordinator is checking two candidate records before the recruiter handoff. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. Find the candidate named “Noah Williams - Support Lead” at “Customer Care” and the candidate named “Leila Haddad - Grants Manager” at “Programs”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Give each record one sentence in the requested order, then add exactly one final sentence comparing the statuses; do not add recommendations.
